data.description:
## Why
Prove the entire architecture end-to-end on the SMALLEST unit before generalizing: a schema
registry holding input+output contracts, a step DECLARING a contract, the editor SURFACING it,
and validation CONSUMING it. This is the walking skeleton that validates the epic's central
claim ("every step kind advertises input/output contract + description, consumed by the editor")
on one scripted op, so design flaws surface in week 1 rather than after the whole model is built.

## What
- Extend the existing schema registry (`src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` `registry()`/`validator()`,
  already `referencing.Registry` + `Draft202012Validator`, 2020-12) so it ALSO resolves INPUT
  schemas by name. Author one input schema file for `fetch_ticket`.
- Add a `StepContract` (`{input_schema, output_schema, description}`) and extend the
  `@register_step` decorator (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py`) to accept
  `input_schema=`/`output_schema=`/`description=`; attach it to `fetch_ticket` in
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/steps.py`. Expose a lookup `contract_for(step_name)`.
- Editor inspector (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` + `editor_assets/`): for a selected node,
  render its CONSUMES (input contract fields) / PRODUCES (output contract fields) + description,
  READ-ONLY.
- Validation (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/lint_refs.py`): a `with:` field mapped to
  `${{ steps.A.outputs.foo }}` is checked that A's output contract declares `foo` (NAME-EXISTENCE
  only here); recognize a distinct `${{ inputs.* }}` namespace for engine-injected vars
  (ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path) and do NOT flag those.

## Scope
In: the four layers above wired through `fetch_ticket` only. Out: other ops (S1), prompts (S4),
deep/runtime validation (S5), authoring/write-back (S6).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fetch_ticket` declares input+output schema names + description via `@register_step`, resolvable from the registry.
- [ ] The editor inspector shows that op's consumes/produces/description (read-only) for a selected node.
- [ ] `lint_refs` flags a `with:` ref to a non-existent upstream output field; `${{ inputs.* }}` is not flagged.
- [ ] An end-to-end test loads a fixture workflow using `fetch_ticket` and asserts: inspector contract output + lint catches a bad ref + passes a good ref.
data.title: Walking skeleton: contract -> inspector -> validation on one scripted op (end-to-end)
